pr_title: feat: let users find account labels for first-class providers (closes AB-000)
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Frontend called old /items endpoint instead of renamed /accounts, and PaginatedItems interface used wrong field name

issues.description: Frontend calls old /items endpoint URL instead of renamed /accounts. The fetchAccountLabel function requests /v1/providers/${providerId}/items but the backend endpoint was renamed to /v1/providers/${provider_id}/accounts, causing a 404 error and silent failure.
PaginatedItems interface uses `items` field but backend PaginatedAccounts model returns data under `accounts`. This causes response.items to be undefined and .find() to throw a TypeError.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 5 potential issues. View 4 additional findings in Devin Review.

pr_title: fix: keyerror for experiments with attachments [LSO-2079]
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Batch endpoint missing _resolve_presigned_attachments call, so Go experiment attachments broken in non-streaming mode

issues.description: Batch endpoint missing _resolve_presigned_attachments. The stream handler was updated to call await _resolve_presigned_attachments(example.attachments) to convert Go-originated {presigned_url, mime_type, storage_url} format into {data, mime_type}, but the batch handler (internal_batch_examples_handler) at lines 162-173 still merges raw attachments directly into inputs without resolving them. This breaks attachments from Go experiments in non-streaming mode.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 1 potential issue. View 4 additional findings in Devin Review.

pr_title: fix(tracer-sessions): remove streaming_rate from Python stat defaults
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Removing streaming_rate default 0.0 from _STAT_SELECT_TO_DEFAULT_VALUE causes streaming_rate to return None instead of 0.0 in API responses

issues.description: Removing schemas.RunStatsSelect.streaming_rate: 0.0 from _STAT_SELECT_TO_DEFAULT_VALUE causes streaming_rate to return None instead of 0.0 in API responses. At stats.py:500-503, the expression `v or _STAT_SELECT_TO_DEFAULT_VALUE.get(...)` uses Python's `or` operator which treats 0.0 as falsy. When streaming_rate is 0.0 (legitimate value meaning 0% streaming) or None (ClickHouse returns NULL when token_run_count = 0), the fallback lookup now returns None instead of 0.0. This changes the API contract — the MultiSessionStats TypedDict declares streaming_rate: float (not Optional[float]).

pr_title: fix(playground): support disabling experiments by tenant
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Disabled-tenant early return bypasses deferred CompleteExperimentBatch, leaving experiments permanently stuck in-progress

issues.description: Disabled-tenant guard at lines 159-165 returns before the defer that calls CompleteExperimentBatch is registered (line 172). Because SkipRetry means asynq won't retry the task, the batch's run count is never reported back to the tracer_session.__progress tracker. completed_run_count will never reach expected_run_count, so end_time is never set and the experiment appears permanently in-progress.

pr_title: fix(fleet): revert "feat(sandbox): Support x service auth in sandboxes" [closes AB-000]
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: ServiceIdentityForge skip applied to write routes but not read routes in claim_handler.go, breaking Forge's ability to read back created claims

issues.description: ServiceIdentityForge is added as a permission-skip identity on CreateClaim (line 80), UpdateClaim (line 88), and DeleteClaim (line 91), but NOT on ListClaims (line 76), GetClaim (line 83), or GetClaimStatus (line 85). A Forge service identity can create a sandbox claim but cannot subsequently read it back or check its provisioning status. The old requireSandboxPermission applied the Forge skip to ALL routes uniformly.

pr_title: chore: update mcp server crud with new vendor id constraint [closes AB-1766]
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: ON DELETE CASCADE silently deletes MCP servers when vendor disconnected, and frontend filter change breaks visibility of existing Arcade MCP servers

issues.description: The migration creates FK fk_mcp_servers_mcp_vendor_id with ON DELETE CASCADE. When a vendor's settings are deleted via DELETE /v1/platform/mcp-vendors/{vendor_slug}/settings, the mcp_vendors row is deleted, which cascades to delete all mcp_servers rows that reference it. Disconnecting from Arcade (or any vendor) will silently destroy all associated MCP server configurations — an irreversible data loss. ON DELETE SET NULL would preserve the servers while clearing the vendor association.

Frontend filter changed from s.url.startsWith(ARCADE_GATEWAY_BASE_URL) to s.vendor_id === 'arcade'. Existing MCP servers created before this PR were added without a vendor_id (column just added in migration with no backfill). These servers will have vendor_id as undefined, so they'll be excluded by the new filter, causing a regression for non-admin users who previously saw Arcade servers.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 2 potential issues. View 5 additional findings in Devin Review.

pr_title: feat(sandbox): add snapshot and claim migrations for Firecracker sandboxes [INF-1813]
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Index on existing table created non-concurrently violating mandatory rule, and missing ON DELETE CASCADE on sandbox_snapshots.tenant_id FK

issues.description: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS host_sandbox_claims_snapshot_id_idx is created non-concurrently and without an autocommit_block(), violating the mandatory AGENTS.md rule: 'Indexes should always be created CONCURRENTLY in autocommit blocks to avoid locking production tables.' The host_sandbox_claims table is an existing production table. The established codebase pattern uses `with op.get_context().autocommit_block(): + CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`.

sandbox_snapshots table's FK on tenant_id uses REFERENCES tenants(id) without ON DELETE CASCADE. All sibling sandbox tables (host_sandbox_templates, host_sandbox_volumes, host_sandbox_claims, host_sandbox_warmpools) use ON DELETE CASCADE. Without it, any attempt to delete a tenant that has sandbox_snapshots rows will fail with a foreign key violation error, breaking tenant cleanup/deletion workflows.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 2 potential issues. View 3 additional findings in Devin Review.

pr_title: chore: add column to mcp-servers for system id for filtering [relates to AB-1766]
label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: FK constraint added inline with ADD COLUMN acquires write-blocking lock on both tables, and docstring Revises comment references wrong parent migration

issues.description: The ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... REFERENCES mcp_vendors(id) ON DELETE CASCADE statement at lines 19-23 adds a foreign key constraint within a transactional ALTER TABLE. In PostgreSQL, adding an FK acquires a ShareRowExclusiveLock on both mcp_servers (the referencing table) and mcp_vendors (the referenced table), which blocks all INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE on both tables for the duration of the transaction. This violates the spirit of the migration rules in AGENTS.md which emphasize avoiding table locks.

The docstring on line 4 says Revises: 7fc05ba22972, but the actual down_revision on line 13 is 144db48d2f90. The revision 7fc05ba22972 is the grandparent (144db48d2f90's own parent). While Alembic uses the variable and ignores the comment, the mismatch is misleading for developers inspecting the migration chain.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 2 new potential issues. View 4 additional findings in Devin Review.

label: incorrect_implementation
label_reason: Missing data-[disabled]: prefix on hover class causes enabled unchecked radio buttons to show disabled hover background

issues.description: The newly appended class data-[state=unchecked]:hover:bg-disabled on line 47 is missing the data-[disabled]: prefix. Every other class in the disabledUnchecked style string is scoped to data-[disabled]:, but this new addition is not, so it applies to all unchecked radio buttons on hover — including enabled ones. This will either override the correct data-[state=unchecked]:hover:bg-elevated-hover from radioButtonRootStateStyles.uncheckedEnabled or create an unpredictable CSS conflict. Since radioButtonRootStateStyles is consumed by both RadioButton and the new RadioCard, this is a regression affecting all radio-style components.

expected_review.raw_review_body: Devin Review found 1 potential issue. View 3 additional findings in Devin Review.
